Object.Name: Computational Performance
Object.Description1: The OMA Computational Performance object provides attributes to monitor the load on various aspects of the system.

Item.Name: Process ID
Item.Description: The ID of the process for which the measurements in this object instance were taken. 0: refers to the computational resources cumulated usage of ALL the Processes / Tasks running on the device 1-100: Reserved 100-99999999: Process ID (can be custom defined by the vendor or taken directly from the Process ID / Task ID on the OS)

Item.Name: Process Name
Item.Description: String Identifying the name of the process for which the statics held in this object refer to. If present , when Resource “Process ID” is set to 0, this resource should read “ALL processes”

Item.Name: Measuring period
Item.Description: Time interval, in seconds. during which counters were gathered and statics calculated (i.e 60s = 1 min, 300s = 5 min, 900s = 15 min rtc..)

Item.Name: CPU Usage
Item.Description: The percentage of time in average that the CPU took processing the non-idle task(s) during the measuring period defined. This is a multi-instance resource, to be used if more than one CPU is present on the device and the vendor intends to provide visibility of its statistics. When multicore CPU architectures are used, Instance resource 0 when used, always refers to the average usage of all cores.

Item.Name: CPU Load
Item.Description: The average of CPU load over the measuring period defined. CPU load is defined as the number of processes using or waiting to use one core at a single point in time. i,e. if the CPU load average is always below 0.8. This indicates that every process that needs to use the CPU can use it instantly without waiting. If the CPU load average is above 1, this indicates that there are processes that need to use the CPU but cannot at the moment due to the unavailability of the CPU. When multicore CPU architectures are used, Instance 0 when used, always refers to the average Load amongst all cores. This is a multi-instance resource, to be used if more than one CPU is present on the device and the vendor intends to provide visibility of its statistics. When multicore CPU architectures are used, Instance resource 0 when used, always refers to the average usage of all cores.

Item.Name: Critical CPU usage Threshold
Item.Description: The threshold for the percentage of CPU usage that once crossed, should be considered a critical event. If a device supports this threshold setting is is expected to also support the “High CPU Usage” resource, once the % threshold set in this resource is crossed “High CPU Usage” resource should be set to 2.

Item.Name: High CPU Usage
Item.Description: High CPU Usage state 0: Low to Normal Usage 1: Intensive CPU usage 2: CPU usage running at capacity

Item.Name: GPU Usage
Item.Description: The percentage of time in average that the GPU took processing non-idle tasks during the measuring period defined.

Item.Name: Volatile Memory usage
Item.Description: The percentage of available volatile memory consumed by the process(s) identified in resource 0.

Item.Name: Non-Volatile Memory usage
Item.Description: The percentage of available non-volatile memory consumed by the process(s) identified in resource 0.

Item.Name: Critical Low Memory Threshold
Item.Description: The threshold for the percentage of available volatile Memory usage that once crossed, should be considered a critical event. If a device supports this threshold setting is is expected to also support the “Low Memory” resource, once the % threshold set in this resource is crossed “Low memory” resource should be set to 2.

Item.Name: Low Memory
Item.Description: High Memory Usage state: 0: Low to Normal Usage 1: Intensive Memory usage 2: Memory usage running at capacity

Item.Name: Timestamp
Item.Description: The timestamp of when the measurement was performed.
